segments.label: Exchanges & Trading Venues
segments.description: Matched markets and order books — where trades actually execute.

segments.label: Brokers & Execution
segments.description: Access intermediaries: retail and institutional brokerage, smart order routing, prime services.

segments.label: Lending, Credit & Rates Markets
segments.description: Balance-sheet markets: repo, margin finance, securities lending, private credit, DeFi lending, stablecoin credit, and collateralized borrow/lend protocols.

segments.label: Market Data & Feeds
segments.description: Real-time and historical price, quote, and reference data vendors.

segments.label: Alternative Data
segments.description: Non-price signals: sentiment, web-scrape, fundamentals crowdsourcing, transactional data.

segments.label: Analytics, Research & Terminals
segments.description: Workstations, charting, factor/quant tooling, and research platforms built on top of data.

segments.label: Prediction & Event Markets
segments.description: Venues for trading contracts on real-world outcomes — elections, economics, sports, weather.

segments.label: Crypto-Native Infrastructure
segments.description: DEX protocols, on-chain analytics, node/indexing, and institutional crypto plumbing.

segments.label: Clearing, Custody & Settlement
segments.description: Post-trade plumbing: central counterparties, depositories, qualified custodians.

segments.label: Indices & Benchmarks
segments.description: Index providers and reference rates the rest of the industry prices against.

segments.label: Asset Management & Strategy Markets
segments.description: The delegated-capital layer: funds, vault protocols, copy trading, and marketplaces that turn trading track records into investable products. Sits on top of the stack — this is the demand side of everything else on the map.

layers.label: L1 — Venue / Liquidity
layers.description: Where the trade clears or matches.

layers.label: L2 — Connectivity / Execution
layers.description: Brokerage, FIX gateways, smart order routing.

layers.label: L4 — Analytics / Applications
layers.description: Research tools, terminals, and signal products on top of data.

asset_classes.label: Crypto Lending
asset_classes.description: On-chain and institutional crypto borrow/lend markets, including overcollateralized money markets and credit pools.

asset_classes.label: Stablecoins
asset_classes.description: Dollar and fiat-linked crypto settlement assets, savings products, and credit primitives.

asset_classes.label: Staking & Yield
asset_classes.description: Staking, liquid staking, restaking, and yield-token markets.

asset_classes.label: Private Credit
asset_classes.description: Direct lending and non-bank private debt.

asset_classes.label: Sports & Event Contracts
asset_classes.description: Tradeable contracts on sports and real-world event outcomes.

asset_classes.label: Collectibles & Art
asset_classes.description: Fractionalized art, cards, and other tangible alternatives.

cost_tiers.label: Free
cost_tiers.description: For venues/data/infra: public data or API access at no cost. For brokers: zero-commission core product. Never refers to trading fees on venues.

cost_tiers.label: Freemium
cost_tiers.description: Meaningful free tier; paid tiers unlock depth or rate limits.

cost_tiers.label: Paid
cost_tiers.description: Subscription required for useful access; self-serve pricing.

statuses.label: Acquired
statuses.description: Still operating, but absorbed into another listed or unlisted entity.

statuses.label: Defunct
statuses.description: Kept for reference — illustrates ecosystem churn.

matrix_cells.description: Semantics for empty cells in the layer-x-segment matrix. 'structural' = definitionally empty (taxonomy artifact or conflict-of-interest wall) — render borderless/inert. 'opportunity' = real-world white space worth annotating — render with accent + tooltip note. Any other empty cell = 'zero entries so far' — render as a normal dim cell.

structural.reason: A data vendor operating a venue would be reclassified as an exchange (single-select segment).
Exchanges *for* data keep failing: data is non-rival and adversely selected, so it resists market-making.
An analytics firm running a venue would be reclassified as an exchange.
Benchmark administrator running a venue trading its own index = manipulation conflict; post-LIBOR benchmark regulation walls this off.
CCP-operated venues exist only inside vertically integrated groups, which classify as exchanges (e.g. CME).
Exchanges are the endpoint of connectivity, not providers of it; venue-sold colocation lives under the venue entry.
Data vendors that execute orders are brokers by definition.
No execution role exists for signal vendors.
Index providers do not touch order flow — by design and regulation.
Alt-data delivery rides general data infrastructure; no segment-specific plumbing layer.
Analytics firms consume plumbing rather than provide it.
Benchmark administration is governance, not market plumbing.

opportunity.note: Filling fast as of mid-2026: PMXT (open-source router), Dome (acquired by Polymarket, Feb 2026), IBKR's regulated-venue router (May 2026), Stand terminal. STILL OPEN: a true prime broker — one account, unified collateral, margin netting across regulated (dollar) and crypto (USDC) venues. Every current router requires funded accounts at each venue.

Partially filled (PMXT hosted history, Stand terminal). STILL OPEN: a neutral, enterprise-grade event-data vendor — Kaiko-for-events with cross-venue tick history, resolution outcomes, and calibration analytics. Dome would have been it; it's venue-owned now.

The deepest remaining gap: no cross-venue clearing or fungibility. The 'same' event on Kalshi vs Polymarket has different resolution criteria and can settle differently; no OCC-for-events nets positions across venues, so every arb carries settlement-mismatch risk and double capital. Whoever builds this owns the column.

legend_flows.description: Two flows run through this map in opposite directions. CAPITAL flows down: savers -> asset managers & vaults -> brokers/execution -> venues -> clearing & custody. DATA flows up: venues emit prices -> data vendors normalize -> analytics interpret -> benchmarks standardize -> capital allocates against them. Most entities are simultaneously demand and supply (an exchange supplies liquidity and demands market-maker capital; BlackRock demands data and supplies Aladdin), which is why demand/supply is drawn as a flow, not stored as a facet.
legend_flows.scope_note: Deliberately out of scope: the primary market (issuers, underwriters, IPO/bond syndication, token launches). This map covers secondary-market structure — where instruments trade, not where they are born.

field_notes.country: Optional ISO-3166 alpha-2 HQ country, shown on cards and searchable. NOT a filter facet — region remains the facet. Omitted for decentralized/global entities and where HQ is uncertain.
field_notes.market_mechanisms: Cross-asset product mechanics. These are not top-level segments: a venue can be a CLOB, AMM, lending pool, or RFQ network while still occupying the same market-structure layer.
field_notes.adapter_shapes: Implementation-oriented integration shape for collectors and product surfaces. Adapter shape describes how to connect, not what business the entity is in.

market_mechanisms.label: Central Limit Order Book
market_mechanisms.description: Continuous bids/offers matched by price-time or venue-specific priority.

market_mechanisms.label: AMM / Pool
market_mechanisms.description: Automated liquidity pool where a formula or invariant quotes against pooled assets.

market_mechanisms.label: RFQ / Dealer Quote
market_mechanisms.description: Request-for-quote or dealer-mediated liquidity rather than a public central book.

market_mechanisms.label: Quote Router / SOR
market_mechanisms.description: Aggregates venues or pools and returns executable routes, slippage, and best-price paths.

market_mechanisms.label: Perp / Derivatives Venue
market_mechanisms.description: Venue for perpetuals, futures, options, or synthetic derivatives exposure.

market_mechanisms.label: Lending Pool / Credit Market
market_mechanisms.description: Borrow/lend venue where collateral, utilization, rates, and liquidation rules define credit.

market_mechanisms.label: Vault / Strategy Product
market_mechanisms.description: Delegated capital wrapper, yield vault, or investable strategy built on underlying venues.

market_mechanisms.label: Oracle / Price Attestation
market_mechanisms.description: Published reference price, index, or data attestation consumed by contracts or risk systems.

market_mechanisms.label: Indexer / Subgraph
market_mechanisms.description: Decoded chain, venue, or transaction state exposed as queryable data.

market_mechanisms.label: Custody / Settlement Network
market_mechanisms.description: Key management, qualified custody, collateral movement, and settlement plumbing.

market_mechanisms.label: Prime Brokerage / Financing
market_mechanisms.description: Institutional trading, credit, financing, collateral management, and multi-venue access.

market_mechanisms.label: Reference / Market Data
market_mechanisms.description: Normalized market data, tick history, identifiers, and benchmark/reference products.

adapter_shapes.label: Market Data Adapter
adapter_shapes.description: Books, trades, quotes, funding, chains, and historical data.

adapter_shapes.label: Quote Adapter
adapter_shapes.description: Executable quote and route requests without necessarily owning canonical venue state.

adapter_shapes.label: Public Read Adapter
adapter_shapes.description: Read-only protocol state: pools, reserves, ticks, gauges, vaults, rates, and positions.

adapter_shapes.label: Venue Adapter
adapter_shapes.description: Venue-native books, markets, funding, orders, fills, or execution surfaces.

adapter_shapes.label: Oracle Adapter
adapter_shapes.description: Price-feed IDs, update payloads, attestations, and oracle health.

adapter_shapes.label: Lending Rate Adapter
adapter_shapes.description: Supply/borrow rates, utilization, collateral factors, liquidations, and credit pool state.

adapter_shapes.label: Custody / Settlement Adapter
adapter_shapes.description: Balances, transfers, wallets, collateral, settlement, and custody network state.

adapter_shapes.label: Portfolio / Vault Adapter
adapter_shapes.description: Vault holdings, strategy NAV, shares, yield, and delegated strategy performance.
